note_data.title: Introduction to Trigonometry
keyPoints: Trigonometry is the study of relationships between angles and sides of triangles
Six trigonometric ratios exist: sine, cosine, tangent, cosecant, secant, and cotangent
In a right-angled triangle, hypotenuse is the longest side opposite to the right angle
Perpendicular is the side opposite to the angle under consideration
Base is the side adjacent to the angle under consideration (excluding hypotenuse)
All trigonometric ratios are dimensionless quantities
sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 is the fundamental trigonometric identity
Complementary angles satisfy: sin θ = cos (90° - θ)
Values of trigonometric ratios for 0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, and 90° must be memorized

definitions.definition: Trigonometry is a branch of mathematics which deals with the relationship between angles and sides of a triangle.
In a right-angled triangle, sine of an angle is defined as the ratio of the perpendicular to the hypotenuse. sin θ = perpendicular/hypotenuse
In a right-angled triangle, cosine of an angle is defined as the ratio of the base to the hypotenuse. cos θ = base/hypotenuse
In a right-angled triangle, tangent of an angle is defined as the ratio of the perpendicular to the base. tan θ = perpendicular/base
Two angles are called complementary if their sum is 90°.

importantQuestions.question: Define trigonometry and write the three basic trigonometric ratios.
importantQuestions.answer: Trigonometry is a branch of mathematics which deals with the relationship between angles and sides of a triangle. The three basic ratios are: sin θ = perpendicular/hypotenuse, cos θ = base/hypotenuse, tan θ = perpendicular/base

importantQuestions.question: In a right-angled triangle ABC, right-angled at B, if AC = 13 cm and BC = 5 cm, find all six trigonometric ratios of angle A.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: AC = 13 cm (hypotenuse), BC = 5 cm (perpendicular to angle A). Using Pythagoras theorem: AB² = AC² - BC² = 169 - 25 = 144, so AB = 12 cm (base). Therefore: sin A = 5/13, cos A = 12/13, tan A = 5/12, cosec A = 13/5, sec A = 13/12, cot A = 12/5

mcqs.question: If sin θ = 3/5, then cos θ is:
If tan θ = 1, then θ equals:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse perpendicular and base when angle position changes
Forgetting to identify hypotenuse correctly (always opposite to right angle)
Not using Pythagoras theorem when one side is missing
Mixing up reciprocal ratios (cosec, sec, cot) with basic ratios
Incorrect memorization of standard angle values (30°, 45°, 60°)
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1-2 short questions in every BSEK paper
Always draw and label the triangle diagram - it carries marks
Memorize the standard angle table - appears in 80% of papers
Practice identifying perpendicular and base for different angle positions
Long questions often combine with Chapter 31 (Heights and Distances)
note_data.keyDiagram: Right-angled triangle ABC with right angle at B, showing hypotenuse AC, perpendicular BC (to angle A), and base AB. Label all sides and mark the angle θ at A. This diagram must be drawn for every trigonometric problem.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define trigonometric ratios and find sin, cos, tan for a given right triangle
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required exact definitions of all six trigonometric ratios with proper mathematical notation
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — tested understanding of complementary angle relationships and co-function identities
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (4 marks) — asked to prove that sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 using textbook method

workedExample.question: In a right triangle ABC, right angled at B, if AB = 3 cm and BC = 4 cm, find all six trigonometric ratios of angle A. (6 marks)
steps: Draw right triangle ABC with right angle at B, AB = 3 cm, BC = 4 cm
Find hypotenuse AC using Pythagoras theorem: AC² = AB² + BC² = 3² + 4² = 9 + 16 = 25, so AC = 5 cm
Identify sides with respect to angle A: opposite = BC = 4 cm, adjacent = AB = 3 cm, hypotenuse = AC = 5 cm
Apply trigonometric ratio definitions: sin A = opposite/hypotenuse = 4/5, cos A = adjacent/hypotenuse = 3/5, tan A = opposite/adjacent = 4/3
Find reciprocal ratios: cosec A = hypotenuse/opposite = 5/4, sec A = hypotenuse/adjacent = 5/3, cot A = adjacent/opposite = 3/4
workedExample.answer: sin A = 4/5, cos A = 3/5, tan A = 4/3, cosec A = 5/4, sec A = 5/3, cot A = 3/4
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct diagram, 1 mark for finding hypotenuse, 1 mark for identifying sides, 3 marks for correct ratios

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for trigonometric ratio definitions.
Many students confuse opposite and adjacent sides. Always clearly identify which side is opposite and which is adjacent to the given angle before calculating ratios.
Avoid using decimal approximations in final answers unless specifically asked. Keep answers in exact fractional form as per Sindh Board marking scheme requirements.
note_data.quickRecap: Trigonometry deals with relationships between sides and angles of right triangles. The six trigonometric ratios are sine, cosine, tangent, cosecant, secant, and cotangent. These ratios are defined as specific fractions involving opposite side, adjacent side, and hypotenuse relative to a given acute angle. The fundamental identity sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 is crucial for solving problems.
